page_content:
ANSC401 Animal Growth and Development for Production Agriculture (3 Credits)

An integration of the physiological, genetic, and nutritional bases of animal growth, development, and body composition with application to livestock production.

Prerequisite: ANSC201.

ANSC410 The Gut Microbiome and its Roles in Health and Disease (3 Credits)

A comprehensive perspective of the role of gut microbiome/microflora in nutrition, metabolism, disease prevention and health issues including farm animal health and food value, and human gastrointestinal health and immunity.

Prerequisite: BSCI223, ANSC212, ANSC327, EPIB301, BSCI222, BSC1421, or NFSC430; or students who have taken courses with comparable content may contact the department.

Credit Only Granted for. ANSC489M or ANSC410.
